Ian Brogan — Summer 2026

I think in markets. I make by hand.

Seventeen, self-taught, and building toward a career in fund management — with a vineyard at the end of the road.

My story See the work
@ianbrogan1227 · @brogan.bites · ianbrogan2@hotmail.com

"Precision in the numbers. Patience in the making."

About

Two worlds, one through-line.

I grew up fascinated by the complex spiderwebs that create businesses — and equally drawn to the patience and craft of making things with your hands.

I've been teaching myself markets since I was ten — reading financial statements, building a personal investment account, learning how funds work. Not because anyone told me to. Because it felt like finding a language I already spoke.

A medically necessary year of independent study became the best thing that happened to me. It gave me time to learn 3D design, printing, woodworking, and graphic production. I built a resin table with my hands. I designed and vinyl-wrapped a custom 3D-printed spoiler shelf. I taught myself the full Adobe suite. I found out that I work best when I get to move between the precise and the tactile.

I was promoted to Lead Studio Director for my school's TV & Film program. I was entrusted with roughly $100,000 to revamp a CTE program from the ground up. I teach advanced cooking because I find the same satisfaction in a properly reduced sauce that I find in a properly constructed financial model.

My ADHD is my greatest asset. It's the engine behind the range. The thing that lets me care deeply about options pricing at 9 AM and be elbow-deep in resin work by 2 PM.

  • Since age 10 Self-taught investor
  • Full mastery Adobe suite
  • ~$100K entrusted CTE budget
  • Promoted Lead Studio Director
Journey

The equity curve of a life.

Every milestone, mapped like a market. The solid line is where I've been. The dashed line is where I'm headed.

View milestones as a list
Ian Brogan's life milestones — age and description
AgeMilestoneDescriptionPhase
10 First investment account Self-taught investor from day one. Read financial statements before I read novels. Past
13.5 Independent study year A medical pivot, not a setback. It became a launchpad for self-teaching design, 3D printing, and craft. Past
14.5 ADHD as the engine Reframed the diagnosis. The range, the intensity, the ability to move between worlds — that's the feature. Past
15.5 Self-taught 2D/3D design & printing Built a resin table. Designed and vinyl-wrapped a custom 3D-printed spoiler shelf. Mastered the full Adobe suite. Past
16.5 Lead Studio Director Promoted to lead director for the school's TV & Film and Digital Media program. Past
17 $100K CTE program revamp Entrusted with roughly $100,000 to overhaul a CTE program launching summer 2026. Past
17.5 Present — Summer 2026 Building toward college, compounding every skill. Present
18.5 College Business Economics + Data Analytics. Cal Poly SLO, UC Davis, or UC Berkeley — the gateway to everything next. Projected
20 BlackRock internship The foot in the door. Learn how real capital moves at scale. Projected
23 Junior data analyst First real career chapter. Data-driven decisions at an institutional level. Projected
27 Senior data analyst Building the expertise and the track record. Projected
32 Fund manager The long goal. Mastery over capital allocation — not just analysis. Projected
42 Compound wealth & mastery Decades of compounding — financial, intellectual, personal. Projected
55 Retire to the vineyard Where the two worlds finally merge. Precision in the growing season. Patience in the aging barrel. Projected

View full portfolio
Investing

I've been reading the market since I was ten.

Not paper trading. Not a simulator. A real account, real decisions, real consequences — and years of learning what not to do.

I started because money felt like a language no one had taught me but everyone seemed to speak. I read Buffett, Klarman, Lynch. I worked through 10-Ks and earnings calls before I had any formal instruction. I wanted to understand how value was created and destroyed.

The journey has been humbling and clarifying in equal measure. I've been wrong in ways that cost me and right in ways I didn't fully deserve. I've learned that discipline — showing up with the same process regardless of noise — matters more than any individual insight.

I'm drawn to the complexity: how geopolitical events ripple through supply chains into earnings, how narrative and reality diverge and eventually reconcile, how the market prices uncertainty differently from risk. The spiderweb structure of it.

The goal: BlackRock internship → junior data analyst → senior data analyst → fund manager. Every step is about building the compounding base — skills, judgment, track record.

Philosophy

  • — Process over prediction — repeatable thinking compounds better than occasional brilliance
  • — Understand before you buy — if you can't explain the thesis simply, you don't own it yet
  • — The long view earns the right to have opinions about the short view
  • — Finance is one part of a larger system — the vineyards, the crafts, the food all feed back in
Skills

Four domains. One disposition.

Finance sharpens the analysis. Design trains the eye. Making builds the patience. Cooking keeps the hands honest.

Finance & Data

  • Investment analysis
  • Financial statements
  • Excel & spreadsheet modeling
  • Markets & funds
  • Data analytics
  • Python (learning)

  • 3D design & printing

  • Vinyl wrapping
  • Crochet

Culinary

  • Advanced pastry & baking
  • French technique
  • Food styling & photography
  • Menu development
Core Advanced Building
Vision

The roadmap is long. That's the point.

I don't want a career. I want a compounding arc — one where every stage builds the foundation for the next.

Where I'm applying

  • 01
    Cal Poly SLO Business Economics + Data Analytics Strong applied-learning culture. Practical foundation.
  • 02
    UC Davis Business Economics + potential Viticulture & Enology minor The vineyard thread runs directly through UC Davis.
  • 03
    UC Berkeley Business / Haas (if eligible) + Data Analytics The academic ceiling. Haas feeds directly into finance.

The arc

  1. Near term College

    Business Economics major. Data Analytics minor. Possibly Viticulture & Enology or Film/Production as a second minor.

  2. Early career BlackRock internship

    Family connection provides the foot in the door. I bring the prep work — years of self-directed market study.

  3. Building Junior → Senior data analyst

    Learn how institutional capital actually moves. Build the quantitative depth the fund management role demands.

  4. The goal Fund manager

    Not just managing returns — mastering the discipline of capital allocation at scale.

  5. The endgame Retire to the vineyard

    Where the two worlds finally merge. Precision in the growing season. Patience in the aging barrel. Craft at the end of a long, compounding arc.

"The vineyard isn't a retirement. It's the proof that you built something worth resting in."

Journal

Notes from the intersection.

Writing about markets, making, and what it looks like to build something from the ground up.

  1. May 1, 2026 makingdesignADHDcraft

    The Year I Built Things

    A medical year away from school sounds like a setback. It turned into the most productive stretch of my life — and an unexpected answer to a question I didn't know I was asking.

    Read →
  2. March 14, 2026 investingmarketsself-taught

    What I Learned by Losing Money at Ten Years Old

    Nobody teaches you that the first lesson in investing is what a bad decision feels like. Mine cost me money I'd saved for months. It also cost me the illusion that this was simple.

    Read →
